title: Belief Sequencing

content.question: What is the exact sequence of beliefs, first, second, third, a customer must adopt before they're ready to pay you?
content.equation: BS = (Σ Belief Confidence × Order Fit) ÷ Breaks
sop: List the 3-5 beliefs a stranger must hold, in order, before they'll buy.
Map your homepage, pitch deck, or sales call against that order and mark every place it skips a step.
Rate Belief Confidence and Order Fit for each belief 1-10, and count your Breaks, the places prospects object or drop off (see calculation, page 5)
Reorder any section that introduces a later belief before an earlier one is settled.
Re-test the sequence with 3 new prospects and see if the same break point still shows up.
content.explainer: You can't sell the solution before the problem. Map the belief ladder and walk them up, one rung at a time.
questions: What is the exact sequence of beliefs, first, second, third, a customer must adopt before they're ready to pay you?
Which belief are you asking people to accept before they've accepted the one before it?
Where in your pitch or page does a prospect's logic actually break, not just lose interest?
content.equation_example: Rate Belief Confidence 1-10 and Order Fit 1-10 for each belief in your sequence, sum the products, then divide by Breaks (the count of places prospects stall or object). Three beliefs score 8×9=72, 7×8=56, and 6×7=42 (sum 170), divided by 2 breaks → BS = 85. Higher BS means beliefs are landing in the right order with few stalls; lower means you're asking for the sale before the logic is built.
content.closing_statement: Sell the belief before you sell the solution.

caption:
Principle Nº 8: Belief Sequencing

Conversion = ordered belief acquisition.

Ask yourself: What is the exact sequence of beliefs, first, second, third, a customer must adopt before they're ready to pay you?
